bbc.title: BBC visits aftermath of Israeli strike on Lebanon that killed family as IDF targets Hezbollah
bbc.description: A relative tells BBC those killed were civilians and not Hezbollah operatives, but the Israel Defense Forces says it was targeting "terrorist infrastructure".

bbc.title: US names six crew killed in refuelling plane crash in Iraq
bbc.description: The aircraft was on a combat mission as part of ongoing US operations against Iran when it crashed in western Iraq on Thursday.

bbc.title: Severe flooding kills 62 in Kenya, as heavy rains continue
bbc.description: Eleven people were rescued overnight after a minibus taxi got stuck in rising floodwaters in Nairobi.

bbc.title: Israeli forces kill Palestinian couple and two of their children in occupied West Bank
bbc.description: Mohammed, aged five, and Othman, aged seven, were killed alongside their parents, the Palestinian health ministry says.

bbc.title: Treasure hunter freed from prison after refusing to turn over shipwreck gold
bbc.description: Tommy Thompson spent more than a decade in prison after refusing to disclose the whereabouts of 500 missing gold coins.

bbc.title: France take title with thrilling last-gasp win over England
bbc.description: France claim a third Six Nations title in the past five years as Thomas Ramos' last-gasp kick decides a 94-point thriller against England in Paris.

bbc.title: Cuban protesters ransack Communist office as energy crisis deepens
bbc.description: Discontent is mounting over rolling blackouts and shortages of food, fuel and medicine, exacerbated by a US blockade.

bbc.title: 'Gruesome' war bets fuel calls for crackdown on prediction markets
bbc.description: Predictions markets have hosted millions of dollars of bets related to the war in Iran.

wsj.title: Palestinians Stream Back to Northern Gaza on Foot
wsj.description: Israel allowed displaced Gazans to begin crossing a military zone that bisects the enclave after a deadlock over hostage releases was broken.

wsj.title: Leading China Property Developer Reports Huge loss, in Sign of Widening Real-Estate Woes
wsj.description: Troubles at Vanke raise questions about the continued spread of the property crisis and whether the Chinese state will step in.

wsj.title: Freed Israeli Hostages Still Had Shrapnel in Their Bodies From Oct. 7 Attack
wsj.description: Some of the women were held alone for extended periods and spent eight months in tunnels, an Israeli medical official said.

wsj.title: Suspected Sabotage of Deep-Sea Cable Triggers First NATO-Led Response
wsj.description: The alliance mounted its first coordinated response to a suspected sabotage campaign against critical infrastructure after another cable was severed in the Baltic Sea.

wsj.title: Rwanda-Backed Rebels Enter Congo's Safe-Haven City
wsj.description: Residents of Goma reported gunfire and shelling after rebels overran Congolese troops. U.N. officials estimated that more than one million displaced people were now inside the city.

wsj.title: Cocaine-Funded Gangs Shake Colombia Years After Peace Pact
wsj.description: Attacks by powerful militias against civilians reflect the state’s inaction eight years after a peace accord removed a powerful rebel group from the field.

wsj.title: Italy Supports Saudi Arabia Joining Fighter-Jet Program, PM Meloni Says
wsj.description: The U.K., Italy and Japan have already partnered on the Global Combat Air Programme, which aims to put a new stealth fighter with supersonic capability in the skies by 2035.

wsj.title: Shippers Wary of Red Sea Routes Despite Houthi Pledge to End Targeting
wsj.description: The world’s top three container operators said they fear instability in Gaza and broader regional tensions mean continued danger.

wsj.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
wsj.description: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

wsj.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
wsj.description: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

wsj.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
wsj.description: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

wsj.title: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
wsj.description: Gain insight on Experian, SoFi Technologies and more in the latest Market Talks covering Financial Services.

wsj.title: Arabica Coffee Prices Hit Record on U.S., Colombia Tariff Spat
wsj.description: Arabica coffee prices hit a record level, as traders digested the withdrawal of President Trump’s threats to impose tariffs and economic sanctions on Colombia.

wsj.title: Swiss franc, Japanese yen Rise as DeepSeek News Boosts Safe Havens
wsj.description: The Yen and Swiss Franc were stronger against the dollar as investors sought safe havens after Chinese start-up DeepSeek’s new AI model hit U.S. tech stocks.

wsj.title: Natural Gas Falls on Shifting Weather Forecasts
wsj.description: U.S. natural gas futures fell after weekend updates took some of the chill out of early February weather forecasts.

wsj.title: Building-Products Distributor QXO Launches Hostile Bid for Beacon
wsj.description: QXO is taking its offer directly to shareholders after being rebuffed on several occasions.

bloomberg.title: Bangladesh Lifts Fuel Curbs Ahead of Nation’s Biggest Festival
bloomberg.description: Bangladesh lifted restrictions on fuel sales, boosting supplies ahead of the Eid-ul-Fitr holidays as well as ensuring there’s enough water to irrigate paddy fields.

bloomberg.title: Key UAE Port Resumes Oil Loadings After Drone Attack, Fire

bloomberg.title: Air China to Resume Beijing-Pyongyang Flights March 30
bloomberg.description: Air China Ltd. will resume direct flights between Beijing and Pyongyang on March 30, according to booking information on travel platform Ctrip.com.

bloomberg.title: Australian Treasurer Says Household Costs to Rise on Oil Price
bloomberg.description: Australian households are likely to face increased cost-of-living pressures, with the inflation rate set to rise above 4.5% as the oil price climbs, Treasurer Jim Chalmers said on Sunday.

bloomberg.title: New UK Far-Right Parties Are Coming to Compete for Farage’s Voters
bloomberg.description: Nigel Farage has stoked a crisis in mainstream UK conservatism by hiving off voters on its right fringe. After a year leading the polls, others are now training that tactic on him.

bloomberg.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskiy called tying Ukraine’s access to €90 billion ($103 billion) in European Union loans to the restoration of the Druzhba pipeline “blackmail.”

bloomberg.title: Zelenskiy Says Ukraine Seeks Drone Deals With US, Gulf Region
bloomberg.description: Ukraine’s president said a drone deal with the US, centered on the exchange of technologies, expertise and production, was possible despite a public rejection from President Donald Trump.

bloomberg.title: Uganda Opposition Leader Flees Country After Months in Hiding
bloomberg.description: Uganda’s main opposition leader Robert Kyagulanyi has left the country after two months in hiding following the disputed presidential and parliamentary polls held on Jan. 15.

aljazeera.description: Thousands in Paris protest military operations in the Middle East. One of more than 85 protests across France.

aljazeera.description: The world's two largest economies trying to hash out trade issues before US President Trump is expected to visit China.

aljazeera.description: The 19-year-old converts his pole position into a historic win to consolidate Mercedes's hold early in the F1 season.
